Notes: Even more OTM won bigger % but higher risk
Lower % but more dollars and safety
Right direction but theta+IV crush killed it
Near ATM captures strong move well
High IV entry = crush even when right
Low IV entry + expansion = bonus gains

Scenario: Delta: How much option moves per $1 move in underlying (0.30 = $0.30 per $1 move)
Gamma: How much delta changes per $1 move (acceleration factor)
Theta: Daily time decay in dollars (always negative for long options)
Vega: Change in option value per 1% change in implied volatility
Delta_Gain = Price_Move × Average_Delta (accounting for gamma acceleration)
1. OTM options can return 900-1500% in explosive moves but require >30% underlying moves
2. Theta decay under 30 DTE becomes exponential - avoid unless scalping
3. Sweet spot appears to be 10-15% OTM with 60-75 DTE for balanced risk/reward
4. ITM options provide 50-200% returns with much higher probability and less stress
5. Rolling strategy can compound gains by resetting theta and maintaining gamma exposure
6. IV crush can destroy gains even when direction is correct
7. Diagonal spreads can be theta-positive while maintaining upside exposure
